subject: Re: Tilt series alignment,
body:
Hey there,

It seems like you have some sort of permissions issue going on rather than
anything to do with WarpTools or IMOD, or maybe you've run out of space on
disk? Unsure

Cheers,

Alister

On Tue, 22 Oct 2024 at 09:38, TSB <tsbrant@umich.edu> wrote:

> Hello,
>
> I'm currently running a practice dataset through the WarpTools workflow. I
> have reached the step of tilt series alignment, for which I am running the
> following:
>
> WarpTools ts_etomo_fiducials \
> --settings warp_tiltseries.settings \
> --angpix 10 \
> --fiducial_size 10 \
> --initial_axis 85.3
>
> Unfortunately, running this gives the following message:
>
> Failed to import alignments:
> Could not find TS_43.xf
>
> This message then repeats for all tilt series. I dug into the log files,
> and found that the batchruntomo.log files contained the following (added in
> tildes myself to shorten the paths):
>
> Batchruntomo started on data set TS_43, Tue Oct 22 12:05:09 2024  [brt11]
> LOG: Invalid Dataset Directory:
> Unwritable directory: ~/practice/virion/warp_tiltseries/tiltstack/TS_43
>
> ERROR: etomo exited with an error status, check:
> ~/.etomologs/etomo_err_Oct-22-120509.log
> ERROR: etomo --fromBRT --directive
> "~/practice/virion/warp_tiltseries/tiltstack/TS_43/batchDirective.adoc"
> --namingstyle 1: exited with status 1
> ABORT SET: etomo setup failed, cannot proceed with this data set
>
> Looking at the etomo_err_Oct-22-120509.log file reveals the same message,
> that there is an Invalid Dataset Directory. This same issue occurs if I try
> using the WarpTools patch tracking tool instead.
>
> I can't seem to determine whether this is an issue with IMOD, with the
> WarpTools wrapper, or something else with our setup. When I ran this step
> with the tutorial apoferritin dataset a couple weeks ago, it ran just fine.
> Does anyone have any suggestions?
>
> --
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups
> "Warp" group.

> .
>
